edges.subject: jdk
edges.evidence: Java language environment (JDK) provides a Collections Framework that hold together several classes and interfaces of the type collection

edges.subject: java collections framework
edges.evidence: Originally, JDK provided the ability to group objects through Arrays (as a native language feature) and Vectors or Hashtables (as utility classes)

edges.subject: each type of java collection
edges.evidence: Each type of Java Collection has a different level of functionality and memory overhead

edges.subject: object
edges.evidence: JVM implements HashMap as an array of HashMap$Entry objects with each object having 4 fields: 1. int hash value computed using a hash function 2. Object key 3. Object value 4. Object next reference to the next HashMap$Entry object as the HashMap is implemented as a linked list

edges.subject: object
edges.evidence: JVM implements HashMap as an array of HashMap$Entry objects with each object having 4 fields: 1. int hash value computed using a hash function 2. Object key 3. Object value 4. Object next reference to the next HashMap$Entry object as the HashMap is implemented as a linked list

edges.subject: search
edges.evidence: Additional data stored in hash-based collections improve the performance of search for items and therefore insertion and deletion

edges.subject: collection framework
edges.evidence: Each type of Java Collection has a different level of functionality and memory overhead

edges.subject: load factor
edges.evidence: The threshold at which rehashing happens along with capacity increase is computed as Capacity x Load Factor and has an initial default value of 12
